senses.definition: an extinct insect of the order Protorthoptera, related to Orthoptera, known from the Upper Carboniferous and Permian periods
examples: Fossils of protorthopterons have been found in early Permian deposits.
Protorthopterons show venational features reminiscent of modern crickets.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling protorthoptera, an extinct group of insects related to Orthoptera
examples: Protorthopterous features are studied in paleontology to understand early wing venation.
The fossil shows protorthopterous characteristics.

senses.definition: the form of a salt with the lowest proportion of base; a salt derived from a protoxide
examples: In chemistry, a protosalt is the salt with the minimal base proportion.
The protosalt forms a starting point for further reactions.

senses.definition: a member of Protosaura or a protosaurian reptile; an extinct early archosauromorph
examples: Protosaurians are studied to understand early archosauromorph evolution.
Fossils of protosaurian species date back to the Permian.

senses.definition: relating to or resembling early science or preliminary scientific ideas; not fully established as science
examples: Protoscientific theories predated modern experimental methods.
Some protoscientific beliefs were later revised by evidence.

senses.definition: a taxonomic grouping with earliest sharks or shark-like fishes in early vertebrate evolution
examples: Protoseelachii encompass early shark-like species from the Paleozoic.
Paleontologists discuss protoselachii when tracing shark ancestry.

senses.definition: a silicate compound formed in proto-chemical or early mineral contexts
examples: Protosilicate minerals appear in early silicate chemistry studies.
The protosilicate phase is of interest in metamorphic rocks.

senses.definition: an early form or precursor of silicon in geological or materials contexts
examples: Protosilicon species are discussed in early solar system materials.
Researchers study protosilicon deposition in ancient rocks.

senses.definition: an archaic or rare term likely referring to an early or proto inner part; not widely used
examples: The manuscript uses protosinner to describe an inner structure.
In modern texts, protosinner is seldom encountered.

senses.definition: an early form of siphon or a proto-siphonous structure in biological contexts
examples: Protosiphon structures appear in primitive invertebrates.
The term protosiphon is found in some historical biological descriptions.

senses.definition: a family name in taxonomy, presumably ancient or historical in usage
examples: Protosiphonaceae was described in early taxonomic works.
The protosiphonaceae family is rarely referenced in modern literature.

senses.definition: characteristic of or resembling protosiphon or its group; proto-siphonous
examples: The protosiphonaceous deposits hint at ancient aquatic life.
Protossiphonaceous features are discussed in paleobiology.

senses.definition: relating to early social behavior; forming or supporting social groups
examples: Protocial behavior in animals helps groups survive.
Protocial tendencies appear in some infant primates.

senses.definition: an initial or primitive solution to a problem; a first attempt at solving something
examples: The protosolution was soon replaced by a better method.
Researchers tested several protosolutions before finding a robust approach.

senses.definition: a preliminary or first spasm; a brief muscular contraction thought to be a precursor
examples: The protospasm indicated the onset of a larger muscular event.
In medical texts, protospasm is discussed as an early sign.

senses.definition: a proto-spatial agent or partner; an archaic or speculative term
examples: The text mentions a protospataire as a hypothetical collaborator.
Scholars debate the meaning of protospataire in ancient manuscripts.

senses.definition: plural of protospataire; hypothetical proto-spatial agents
examples: Ancient texts reference protospataires in multiple passages.
Scholars discuss the possible roles of protospataires.

senses.definition: an ancient or proto-spathite figure or instrument; a historical term
examples: The protospathaire is described in relic texts.
Researchers seek clearer usage of protospathaire.

senses.definition: plural of protospathaire; proto-spathite figures or instruments
examples: The protospathaires described in ancient scribal records are debated.
Protospathaires may refer to multiple ritual objects.

senses.definition: a proto-spatharius; an ancient title or role in historical contexts
examples: The protospatharius is listed among early medieval officials.
Scholars analyze the duties of protospatharius in chartered records.

senses.definition: an extinct genus of papery or armored reptile; a paleontological term
examples: Protosphargis fossils help illustrate Triassic reptile diversity.
The genus Protosphargis was described in older taxonomic works.

senses.definition: an extinct group within early vertebrate evolution, part of the larger group Spondyli
examples: Proto­spondyli are discussed in studies of early tetrapod evolution.
Fossils attributed to protospondyli inform phylogenetic debates.

senses.definition: a spore produced early in a life cycle before maturity; a primitive spore
examples: Protospore formation occurs during initial spore development.
Protospore may be discussed in studies of fungal or algal life cycles.

senses.definition: a very young star that has just formed and is still gathering mass
examples: The protostar will eventually become a main-sequence star.
Astronomers studied how protostars heat surrounding gas.

senses.definition: plural of protostar; very young stars in formation
examples: Protostars are common in star-forming regions.
Observatories capture light from distant protostars.
